description: Regression eval for the v1.5 double-comment anti-pattern. The model must post exactly one structured triage comment (no recap 'Triage complete' follow-up). Fixture body is the verbatim issue #30 (zombie wiring in apps/desktop/src/main/projects.ts).

issue.title: [BUG] projects.ts stub: closeProject() no-op wired into before-quit lifecycle
issue.body:
## Bug Description

`apps/desktop/src/main/projects.ts` is a 22-line stub file that exports two no-op functions, `openProject()` and `closeProject()`. The functions are placeholders for the F5 (Projects page) feature, marked with `TODO(PR5)`.

The stub is not isolated: `closeProject()` is imported at `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts:8` and called from the `before-quit` lifecycle hook (line 135):

```ts
app.on('before-quit', () => {
  closeProject()  // no-op
  closeSqlite(handle)
})
```

This creates a misleading lifecycle: a reader of `main/index.ts` would reasonably assume the app properly closes the active project on quit. In reality, no project state is touched, no cleanup occurs, and the call is dead.

The `// TODO(PR5):` comment at the top of the file acknowledges this is a placeholder for the F5 (Projects page) feature. The current state is "zombie code" — present, wired, but inert.

## Severity / Priority

p3: low — known technical debt item with an explicit TODO, not a user-facing bug.

## Impact Assessment

- Affected users: developers and reviewers only; no end-user-visible behavior
- Frequency: every app quit (`closeProject` runs on every shutdown)
- Workaround available: yes — the dead call is harmless in practice, but it misleads readers and will silently no-op if someone adds a state-tracking project model in F5 and forgets the close hook already exists
- Risk: if F5 lands without revisiting this file, the "close" path will still be a no-op, and the database row for the active project will not be cleared (since none of the F5 project state is in place yet)

## Reproduction Steps

1. Open the desktop app and quit it
2. Observe: `closeProject()` is called from `before-quit` (line 135 of `main/index.ts`)
3. Observe: the function body is empty (line 21-22 of `projects.ts`)
4. Expected: the function either implements close logic, or the wiring in `main/index.ts` is removed until F5 lands
5. Actual: a wired no-op runs on every quit

## Reproduction Frequency

Always (100%) — every quit calls the no-op.

## OS / Environment

- macOS 14.4, Windows 11, Ubuntu 24.04 (all reproduce)
- Electron 35.x
- pnpm 9 / Node 24

## Component Affected

- `apps/desktop/src/main/projects.ts` (stub)
- `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts` (lifecycle hook)

## Relevant Documentation

- The F5 (Projects page) design doc is referenced in `docs/architecture/feature-projects.md` — not yet landed.

## Suggested Fix (one of)

- **Option A (recommended):** Remove the import at `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts:8` and the `closeProject()` call in the `before-quit` hook. Keep `projects.ts` with the `// TODO(PR5)` so F5 knows the seam exists, or delete `projects.ts` entirely until F5 lands.
- **Option B:** Make `closeProject()` a true no-op (e.g. `export function closeProject(): void { /* no-op until F5 */ }`) and add an ESLint rule or a comment that flags any F5 author who touches it without providing an implementation.

Option A is preferred because it removes the dead code and the misleading call site; the F5 PR can re-introduce both, this time with a real implementation.

expected.rationale: Regression fixture for the v1.5 double-comment fix. Mirrors issue #30 — the model classifies the dead-code wiring as type:refactor (no observable defect; the [BUG] title prefix and PR framing lean toward type:bug but the runtime behavior matches the TODO(PR5) intent, so refactor is the correct label). Labels p3:low + effort:xs stand. The model must post exactly one structured triage comment (no 'Triage complete' recap).
